Alternatively, recall that `(X^{τ n})_i ω = X_{min i (τ n ω)} ω`. When `i ≤ τ n ω`, this is `X_i ω`.

    -- by considering the localizing sequence and stopping times.
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      obtain ⟨τ, hτ, h₄⟩ := h₂
      have h₅ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (fun x => (τ 0 ω).1 (X x ω)) := by
        -- Use the localizing sequence to get the desired property
        have h₆ := h₄ 0
        have h₇ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (fun x => (τ 0 ω).1 (X x ω)) := by
          filter_upwards [h₆] with ω h₈
          exact h₈
        exact h₇
      -- Simplify the expression to get the right continuity of the original process
      have h₈ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
        filter_upwards [h₅] with ω h₉
        have h₁₀ : (τ 0 ω).1 = X := by
          -- Prove that the stopped process is the original process
          have h₁₁ := (hτ.2) 0
          have h₁₂ := (hτ.2) 0
          simp_all [stoppingTime_eq]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.indicator]) <;>
          (try aesop)
        rw [h₁₀] at h₉
        exact h₉
      exact h₈
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
    have h₂ : Locally (fun X ↦ ∀ ω, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω)) 𝓕 X P := hX
    -- Extract the localizing sequence and the properties from h₂
    obtain ⟨τ, hτ, hτ'⟩ := h₂
    -- Use the fact that the stopped processes are right-continuous and τ tends to infinity
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      -- Use the fact that τ tends to infinity to deduce that X is right-continuous a.e.
      filter_upwards [hτ.tendsto_top] with ω hω
      -- For each ω, use the fact that τ n tends to infinity to get the right-continuity of X · ω
      have h₄ : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
        -- Use the fact that the stopped processes are right-continuous and τ tends to infinity
        -- to deduce the right-continuity of X · ω
        have h₅ : ∀ n, Function.RightContinuous (fun i : ι => X (min i (τ n ω)) ω) := by
          intro n
          have h₆ : Function.RightContinuous ( (processStoppedAt τ n X) · ω ) := hτ' n ω
          convert h₆ using 1
          <;> simp [processStoppedAt, min_comm]
          <;> congr
          <;> ext i
          <;> simp [min_comm]
        -- Use the fact that τ tends to infinity to deduce the right-continuity of X · ω
        have h₆ : Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => (τ n ω : WithTop ι)) atTop (𝓝⊤ : Filter (WithTop ι)) := by simpa using hω
        -- Use the properties of the stopped processes and the fact that τ tends to infinity
        -- to deduce the right-continuity of X · ω
        sorry
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₀ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
    have h₁ := hX.tendsto_top
    have h₂ := hX.mono_left (fun Y _ ↦ by
      simpa [Pi.rightContinuous] using hX.monotone Y)
    -- Extract the localizing sequence and its properties
    obtain ⟨τ, hτ, hτ_prop⟩ := hX
    have h₃ := hX.stoppedProcess
    -- Use the fact that the property holds for the stopped process to deduce it for the original process
    have h₄ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      filter_upwards [hτ.tendsto_top] with ω hω
      have h₅ : ∀ n, Function.RightContinuous (fun x => (hτ.stoppedProcess n ω) x ω) := by
        intro n
        have h₆ := hτ_prop n
        -- The stopped process is right continuous by the local property
        simp_all [Pi.rightContinuous, hτ.isStoppingTime]
        <;> aesop
      -- Use the fact that the stopped process converges to the original process to deduce the right continuity
      have h₆ : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
        -- Use the fact that the stopped process converges to the original process to deduce the right continuity
        have h₇ : ∀ n, (hτ.stoppedProcess n ω) = (hτ.stoppedProcess n ω) := by simp
        have h₈ : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
          -- Use the fact that the stopped process converges to the original process to deduce the right continuity
          have h₉ : ∀ n, (hτ.stoppedProcess n ω) = (hτ.stoppedProcess n ω) := by simp
          -- Use the fact that the stopped process converges to the original process to deduce the right continuity
          exact by
            -- Use the fact that the stopped process converges to the original process to deduce the right continuity
            have h₁₀ := h₅ 0
            simp_all [Pi.rightContinuous]
            <;> aesop
        exact h₈
      exact h₆
    exact h₄
  exact h₀

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
    -- Use the fact that the localizing sequence exists and the property holds locally
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∃ n : ℕ, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      -- For each ω, there exists some n such that the property holds for the stopped process
      have h₃ := hX.exists_stop
      -- Use the fact that the property holds locally to find such an n for each ω
      have h₄ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∃ n : ℕ, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
        filter_upwards [h₃] with ω h₅
        -- For each ω, there exists some n such that the property holds for the stopped process
        have h₆ : ∃ n : ℕ, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
          -- Use the fact that the property holds for the stopped process to find such an n
          have h₇ := h₅.1
          have h₈ := h₅.2
          have h₉ : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
            -- The stopped process is the same as the original process since τ n ω = ∞
            simp_all [Function.RightContinuous]
            <;>
            aesop
          exact ⟨0, h₉⟩
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    -- Use the fact that for each ω, there exists such an n to conclude the proof
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      filter_upwards [h₂] with ω h₄
      -- For each ω, there exists some n such that the property holds for the stopped process
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := h₄
      -- The property holds for the original process as well
      exact hn
    exact h₃
  -- The property holds almost everywhere
  exact h₁

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      -- Extract a localizing sequence from the locally hypothesis
      rcases hX with ⟨τ, hτ_is_localizing, hτ_property⟩
      -- The localizing sequence is not directly needed here, but we need to use the fact that `hτ_property` gives us the right continuity almost surely
      have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
        -- Since `hX` is a Locally statement, we use the fact that the property is satisfied for the stopped process
        have h₄ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
          -- We use the fact that the property is satisfied for the stopped process to get the desired result almost surely
          have h₅ : ∀ᵐ (ω : Ω) ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
            -- The localizing sequence ensures that the property holds on a set of full measure
            filter_upwards [hτ_property 0] with ω hω
            -- Since the property holds for the stopped process, we can directly use it
            exact by
              simpa using hω 0
          exact h₅
        exact h₄
      exact h₃
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

have h_main : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
    obtain ⟨τ, hτ, hτ'⟩ := hX
    have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      have h₂ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ⊤ ∈ {i : WithTop ι | Function.RightContinuous (X · ω)} := by
        have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∀ i : ℕ, (τ i ω) ∈ {i : WithTop ι | Function.RightContinuous (X · ω)} := by
          filter_upwards [hτ'.tendsto_top] with ω hω
          have h₄ : ∀ i : ℕ, (τ i ω) ∈ {i : WithTop ι | Function.RightContinuous (X · ω)} := by
            intro i
            specialize hτ' i
            have h₅ := hτ'
            have h₆ : (X · ω) ∈ {X : ι → E | ∀ ω, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω)} := by
              simpa using h₅ ω
            simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at h₆
            have h₇ : ∀ ω, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := h₆
            have h₈ : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := h₇ ω
            simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
            <;>
            aesop
          exact by
            have h₅ := h₄
            simp_all
        filter_upwards [h₃] with ω hω i
        simp_all [le_top, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        aesop
      have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
        filter_upwards [h₂] with ω hω
        by_cases h : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω)
        · exact h
        · exfalso
          have h₄ := hω
          simp [h] at h₄
          <;>
          aesop
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
  exact h_main

obtain ⟨τ, hτ, sτ⟩ := hX
  have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∃ (x : ℕ), sτ x ω := by
    have h₂ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∃ (n : ℕ), sτ n ω := by
      have h₃ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, sτ 0 ω := by
        exact hτ.tendsto_top 0
      have h₄ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, ∃ (n : ℕ), sτ n ω := by
        filter_upwards [h₃] with ω hω
        exact ⟨0, hω⟩
      exact h₄
    exact h₂
  filter_upwards [h₁] with ω hω
  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := hω
  have h₂ := hx
  simp only [Function.comp_apply] at h₂ ⊢
  exact h₂

have h₁ : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
    obtain ⟨τ, hτ, hτ'⟩ := hX
    filter_upwards [hτ'.1] with ω hω
    -- Since hX is a local property, we can use the stopping time sequence to extract the desired result.
    have h₂ : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
      -- Use the fact that the localized property holds for the stopping time sequence.
      have h₃ : Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by
        -- Extract the right continuity from the local property.
        have h₄ : ∀ ω, Function.RightContinuous (X · ω) := by simpa using hω

    exact h₂
  exact h₁
